# Spec: generate-sbom.md §Recursive Discovery, error handling rules
# Rules (per spec):
#   - File without `schema` marker -> SILENT SKIP.
#   - File with `schema: interlynk/...` but malformed -> HARD ERROR.
#   - Unknown schema version -> HARD ERROR.
#
# EXPECTED GAP: pkg/generate/gsbom/parser.go emits errors that are appended to
# gsbom.Generate's `warnings` slice rather than aborting. Actual behaviour:
# schema-less, malformed, and unknown-version files all produce warnings and the
# run completes successfully. This is *wrong on both sides*:
#   - "silent skip" is not silent (warning is printed), and
#   - "hard error" is not an error.

mkdir a
mkdir b
mkdir c
mv real.json .components.json
mv noschema.json a/.components.json
mv malformed.json b/.components.json
mv badver.json c/.components.json

# Current behaviour: succeeds, prints warnings for all 3 problems.
exec sbomasm generate sbom -c meta.yaml -r . -o out.json
# stderr pattern applies to the most recent exec
stderr 'missing schema marker'
stderr 'unexpected end of JSON'
stderr 'invalid schema'

exec jq -e '[.components[].name] == ["real"]' out.json

-- meta.yaml --
app:
  name: app
  version: 1.0.0
  primary_purpose: application
  supplier:
    name: Acme
  license:
    id: MIT
-- real.json --
{
  "schema": "interlynk/component-manifest/v1",
  "components": [
    { "name": "real", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "library", "license": "MIT",
      "supplier": {"name": "a"},
      "hashes": [{"algorithm": "SHA-256", "value": "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"}],
      "external_references": [{"type": "distribution", "url": "https://x"}]
    }
  ]
}
-- noschema.json --
{
  "components": [
    { "name": "notours", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "library" }
  ]
}
-- malformed.json --
{
  "schema": "interlynk/component-manifest/v1",
  "components": [
    { "name": "oops"
-- badver.json --
{
  "schema": "interlynk/component-manifest/v99",
  "components": []
}
